hypothesis: An Ag gas-diffusion electrode engineered with hierarchical macropores (10–100 µm) and mesopores (2–50 nm) and ~30 wt% PTFE hydrophobicity will enable operation at 250 mA cm−2 total current density in the 1.5 M KHCO3 / 0.75 M K2CO3 electrolyte to produce a CO partial current density >120 mA cm−2 while maintaining CO2 utilization >60%, thereby increasing reactor yield to >72 mA cm−2.
